title: MSD Gold Streptavidin Antibody Preparation and Plate Run Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aedmh","text":"This protocol describes how to conjugate antibodies and run the Meso Scale Discovery (MSD) Sandwich enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) on MSD GOLD 96-well Small Spot Streptavidin SECTOR Plates. This protocol is adapted from MSD GOLD™ Streptavidin Plate and Avidin Plates Quick Guide 1 and MSD GOLD™ SULFO-TAG NHS-Ester Conjugation Quick Guide 2 and optimized for C9orf72 dipeptide repeat detection from human iPSC derived neurons.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Chill PBS (or MSD Conjugation Buffer) and ultrapure water On ice .
Equilibrate Zeba Spin Desalting Columns, MSD Storage Buffer, and Sulfo-NHS-LC-Biotin at Room temperature .
Use one Zeba column per 70 µL of antibody.
In order to both biotinylate and sulfo-tag the antibody, you need at least 140 µL of antibody at an optimal concentration of 1.0 mg/mL .

Note on planning when to biotinylate your antibodies : Before performing this step, plan to tag multiple antibodies at the same time to save both money and reagents. Calculate how much Sulfo-NHS-LC-Biotin you need per antibody, using the following formula: 1,000 × ([Concentration of antibody in mg/mL]/ 150,000 Da) × 20 × 70 µL of antibody = nmol of Biotin needed This nmol of Biotin needed divided by 0.5 nmol/μL Biotin reagent = μL of Sulfo-NHS-LC Biotin needed See attached examples at the end of this document
It still possible to move forward with a less concentrated sample.
Dilute the antibodies with ice-cold PBS if necessary.
Remove the Zeba columns' bottom closure and loosen the cap. Do not remove the cap.
Place the column in a collection tube to remove the storage buffer.
Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 1 : Add 300 µL of PBS (or MSD Conjugation Buffer) to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 2 : Add 300 µL of PBS (or MSD Conjugation Buffer) to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 3 : Add 300 µL of PBS (or MSD Conjugation Buffer) to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 3 minutes . Empty collection tube.
Change collection tube to a clean Eppendorf tube for sample recovery. Label one Eppendorf tube/sample for each sample to be biotinylated or sulfo-tagged.
Pipette 70 µL of the antibody to the spin column.
Spin at 1500 x g, °C, for 3-4 minutes.
Save the eluent On ice . The eluent is the buffer exchanged antibody.
Add 180 µL of ultrapure H 2 O to the 1 mg vial to Sulfo-NHS-LC-Biotin.
Dilute the Sulfo-NHS-LC-Biotin by adding 10 µL of the stock to 190 µL of cold water. Once formed, this is highly unstable and should be used immediately.
Add the calculated volume of diluted reconstituted Sulfo-NHS-LC-Biotin to each antibody.
Let the antibody and biotin incubate at Room temperature for 02:00:00 in the dark.

Note on planning when to sulfo-tag : Before performing this step, plan to tag multiple antibodies at the same time to save both money and reagents. Calculate how much SULFO-TAG NHS-Ester you need per antibody, using the following formula: 1,000 × ([Concentration of antibody in mg/mL]/ 150,000 Da) × 20 × 70 µL of antibody = nmol of Sulfo-Tag reagent needed This nmol of Sulfo-Tag needed divided by 3.0 nmol/µL = µL of sulfo-tag ester solution needed See attached examples at the end of this document
Add 50 µL of ice-cold ultrapure H 2 O to the 150 nmol SULFO-TAG NHS-Ester Vial to make a 3 nmol/μL solution. Once formed, this is highly unstable and should be disposed.
Vortex the solution lightly.
Add the calculated volume of diluted reconstituted SULFO-TAG NHS-Ester reagent to each antibody.
Let the antibody and SULFO-TAG NHS-Ester incubate at Room temperature for 02:00:00 in the dark.
Equilibrate Zeba Spin Desalting Columns, MSD Storage Buffer at Room temperature .
Place the column in a collection tube to remove the storage buffer.
Remove the columns' bottom closure and loosen the cap Do not remove the cap.
Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 1 : Add 300 µL of MSD Conjugate Storage Buffer to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 2 : Add 300 µL of MSD Conjugate Storage Buffer to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 3 : Add 300 µL of MSD Conjugate Storage Buffer to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 3 minutes . Empty collection tube.
Change collection tube for sample recovery.
Pipette 70 µL of the unpurified biotinylated antibody to the spin column.
Spin at 1500 x g, °C, for 3-4 minutes.
Save the eluent On ice . The eluent is the biotinylated antibody. It is stable at 4 °C for 1 year.
Equilibrate Zeba Spin Desalting Columns, MSD Storage Buffer at Room temperature .
Remove the columns' bottom closure and loosen the cap. Do not remove the cap.
Place the column in a collection tube to remove the storage buffer.
Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 1 : Add 300 µL of MSD Conjugate Storage Buffer to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 2 : Add 300 µL of MSD Conjugate Storage Buffer to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute . Empty collection tube.
Wash 3 : Add 300 µL of MSD Conjugate Storage Buffer to the column. Spin at 1500 x g, °C, 3 minutes . Empty collection tube.
Change collection tube for sample recovery.
Pipette 70 µL of the unpurified SULFO-TAG NHS-Ester tagged antibody to the spin column.
Spin at 1500 x g, °C, for 3-4 minutes.
Save the eluent On ice . This is the SULFO-TAG NHS-Ester tagged antibody. It is stable at 4 °C for 1 year.
Dilute biotinylated capture antibodies in 1x DPBS to your desired concentration.
Add 30 µL of the diluted antibody to the corner of each well of the MSD 96 Streptavidin Small. Spot Plate according to the plate format. IMPORTANT : From this point forward, never let the plate dry.
Tap the plate on its edges to ensure the antibody is spread evenly across the well.
Store in the plate at 4 °C to incubate Overnight without shaking.
Tap out the plate to dispose of the capture antibody.

Add 150 µL /well of Blocking Solution (3% Blocker A (or BSA) + PBS) per well. %s %s 3% Blocker A in 1x PBS (Store at 4°C) For 100 mL Blocker A 3 g 1X PBS 100 mL (Stir or shake overnight at 4°C to make sure it is dissolved completely.)
Seal the plate and incubate at Room temperature with shaking at 750 rpm, °C, 1 hour .
Prepare the lysate samples according to plate layout, by diluting the protein samples in the lysate buffer to the desired lysate concentrations.
Tap out the plate.
Wash 1x with 150 µL /well of PBS - T (0.05% Tween).
Discard the wash solution, without letting the plate dry.
Add 25 µL /well of the lysate to the target wells. Add lysate to the bottom corner of the wells.
Seal the plate and incubate at Room temperature with shaking at 750 rpm, °C, for 1-2 hours.
While the lysates incubate, prepare the detection antibodies (your sulfo-tagged antibodies) in 1% MSD Blocker A in 1x DPBS.
Discard the lysate solution. Do not let the plate dry, but remove the excess solution.
Wash with PBS - T (0.05% Tween).
Discard the wash solution, but do not let the plate dry.
Add 25 µL /well of sulfo-tag antibodies (in 1% Blocker A in DPBS) to the plate layout.
Seal the plate and incubate at Room temperature with shaking at 750 rpm, °C, 1 hour .
Tap out the plate.
Wash with PBS - T (0.05% Tween).
Tap out the plate.
Add 150 µL /well of Read Buffer A using reverse pipetting to avoid making bubbles. IMPORTANT : Ensure there is no plastic wrap, tape, or parafilm on the plate.
Read the plate immediately.
Wash with 150 µL /well of PBS - T (0.05% Tween). (1/3)
Wash with 150 µL /well of PBS - T (0.05% Tween). (2/3)
Wash with 150 µL /well of PBS - T (0.05% Tween). (3/3)
Wash with 150 µL /well of PBS - T (0.05% Tween). (1/3)
Wash with 150 µL /well of PBS - T (0.05% Tween). (2/3)
Wash with 150 µL /well of PBS - T (0.05% Tween). (3/3)

This section outlines sample calculations for the biotinylation and sulfo­ tagging of antibodies for the lVISD assay, as described in the protocol above. Relevant notes, using Poly-GR 1VIABN778 and Poly-PR ABN1354 antibodies as references: 0.5mg/mL is the concentration of the antibody 150,000Da is the protein weight for IgG protein 20:1 is the challenge ratio 70 pL is the volume of the protein solution
Poly-GR MABN778 and Poly-PR ABN1354 4.67 nmol of sulfo-NHS-LC Biotin required
9.34 µL of sulfo-NHS-LC Biotin stock solution .
Poly-GA MABN889 3.08 nmol of sulfo-NHS-LC Biotin required
6.16 µL of sulfo-NHS-LC Biotin stock solution.
Poly-GR MABN778 and Poly-PR ABN1354 4.67 nmol of sulfo-tag reagent required.
1.56 µL of sulfo-tag ester solution .
Poly-GA MABN889 3.08 nmol of sulfo-tag reagent required.
1.03 µL of sulfo-tag ester solution.
Seal the plate with parafilm to avoid loss of antibody due to evaporation.
It is only needed to buffer exchange your antibodies if they are in buffers with preservatives such as sodium azide or EDTA or in buffers that contain primary amines or glycerol.
